data.title: Soros Capital takes new Nvidia, Chipotle stakes, exits Alibaba, Microsoft, among top Q4 moves
data.description:
Soros Capital Management opened new positions in Nvidia (NVDA), with 207K shares, Chipotle Mexican Grill (CMG), with 117K shares, and SoFi Technologies (SOFI), with 516K shares, during the last quarter of 2024, according to a 13F filing issued on Friday.

Conversely, the investment fund founded by billionaire investor George Soros sold its stakes in Alibaba (BABA), Microsoft (MSFT) and Ulta Beauty (ULTA).

The firm boosted its holdings in Amazon (AMZN), to 165K shares from 106K shares, Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM), to 152K from 105K, and Core Scientific (CORZ), to 1.16M from 862K, the filing showed.

Decreased stakes include Uber Technologies (UBER), to 8K shares from 140K shares, and Danaher (DHR), to 3K from 21K.

data.title: Sarepta closes on $600M revolving credit facility
data.description:
• Sarepta Therapeutics (NASDAQ:SRPT) has closed on a $600M senior secured revolving credit facility made possible with a bank syndicate.
  • JP Morgan Chase (JPM) served as lead arranger and joint bookrunner.
  • The credit facility "supplements our strong balance sheet and allows for contingent liquidity as we execute our strategic plan to achieve our Sarepta 2030 objectives," CFO Ian Estepan said in a news release.

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P, and Dow are muted, but Wall Street set to snap two-week losing streak
data.description:
The major averages on Friday were little changed, but Wall Street is on track to snap a two-week losing streak. The past few days were dominated by President Donald Trump's remarks on tariffs and by economic data on inflation.

Early on and the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was +0.1%, the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was +0.1%, and the Dow (DJI) was even.

Tariffs took center stage this week, with Trump on Monday announcing 25% levies on all steel and aluminum imports into the U.S. On Thursday, Trump signed a memorandum that tasked his administration to investigate current trade relations and propose reciprocal tariffs on a country-specific basis.

Still, the fact that Trump did not immediately put in effect the tit-for-tat tariffs and was scant on details, helped market sentiment and drove Wall Street more than 1% higher in Thursday's session. A favorable producer inflation report for January also helped.

On the surface, the data showed that the M/M increase in the headline producer price index (PPI) was higher than expectations, while the core PPI came in-line. However, digging deeper, the components in the PPI that directly feed into the core personal consumption expenditures (PCE) price index came in soft for January. That bodes well for the Federal Reserve's favorite inflation gauge.

The PPI update helped overcome the shock provided by hotter-than-expected consumer price index (CPI) readings on Wednesday.

"It was a love-in for markets yesterday with bonds and equities rallying following better-than-expected inflation data and news that details on reciprocal tariffs would not come before April. This led to a revival of hopes that the Fed would still cut rates this year, particularly after the more hawkish fears post the upside surprise in the CPI report the previous day," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

Treasury yields slipped on Friday after January retail sales came in significantly worse than expected. The 10-year yield (US10Y) dipped 6 basis points to 4.47%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) was also down 6 basis points to 4.26%.

After the closing bell, 13F filings will be in focus - regulatory disclosures by major funds of their quarterly equity ownership changes.

data.title: Norsk Hydro closed four extrusion plants in 2024 on weak EV demand
data.description:
Norsk Hydro (OTCQX:NHYDY) closed four extrusion plants in the U.S., U.K., France and Lithuania to reduce costs in the unit that was affected by weak electric vehicle sales, the aluminum producer said after it posted a Q4 profit miss on Friday.

The closures, which were carried out throughout 2024 and led to a cut of more than 900 full-time jobs, are not related to U.S. tariffs, CEO Eivind Kallevik told Reuters.

However, "as the U.S. is a major aluminum importer, tariffs will likely raise premiums and costs, with Midwest premiums already surging to the highest levels since April 2023," Norsk Hydro (OTCQX:NHYDY) said in its earnings statement.

Norsk Hydro (OTCQX:NHYDY) said the extrusions business is little affected by the tariffs, since it relies mainly on domestic raw materials and typically passes higher LME prices and premiums on to customers; the unit, which makes aluminum extrusions used in auto production, is instead suffering from weak EV demand, especially in Germany.

Norsk Hydro (OTCQX:NHYDY) reported Q4 adjusted EBITDA more than doubled to 7.7B Norwegian crowns (~$690M) but missed the 8.28B crowns analyst consensus estimate.

The company said it expects underlying demand for its products to gradually improve into 2025 due to lower interest rates.

data.title: Informatica slumps after rocky Q4 sparks wave of downgrades
data.description:
Informatica (NYSE:INFA) shares tumbled around 35% premarket on Friday after the data management company was hit with a string of downgrades following its weak quarterly results.

Baird downgraded Informatica (NYSE:INFA) to Neutral, citing weaker trends and reduced visibility. Informatica's Q4 results highlighted a deceleration in cloud ARR growth, the brokerage said, with cloud subscription ARR up 34.1% Y/Y to $827.3M vs. 36.1% growth in Q3. The company now forecasts Q1 cloud subscription ARR growth of 29.6% and 25.1% for the full year 2025 vs. Baird’s prior 31.0% full-year estimate.

Operational challenges in renewals and accounting adjustments for cloud migrations also impacted short-term results. Q4 revenue fell 3.8% Y/Y to $428.3M, missing analysts’ consensus estimates of $28.74M.

“INFA reported weaker Q4 results, driven by weaker renewals and higher (partial) churn across the company's on-premise and cloud segments. The weaker trends also negatively impacted 2025 revenue and profitability guidance, including cloud ARR growth, the central focus of our prior positive thesis," Baird said.

RBC Capital Markets downgraded Informatica (INFA) to Sector Perform from Outperform, while lowering its price target from $35 to $19.

“INFA reported a messy/confusing qtr where results missed expectations partially due to sales execution issues around cloud renewals as we struggle to clearly understand what really went wrong. While estimates could be de-risked and the LT opportunity still looks interesting, we believe it could take multiple quarters of improved execution to regain investor confidence,” RBC analysts said in a note.

Informatica (INFA) has also been cut to Equal-Weight at Wells Fargo, while Scotiabank, Truist, Cantor and UBS cut their price targets on the stock to $27, $34, $29 and $30, respectively.

INFA shares were down over 34% premarket on Friday to $16.59.

data.title: AMC Networks misses on main metrics in Q4 as costs swell
data.description:
AMC Networks (NASDAQ:AMCX) reported a top-and-bottom line miss for its fourth quarter results on Friday.

The company said subscription revenues dipped 4% to $314M due to declines in the linear subscriber universe, while streaming revenues rose 8% to $156M from last year.

Free cash flow for the company fell to $37.6M in Q4 from $66M for the same period last year, while operating loss swelled to $254.2M from a loss of $11M last year.

AMC recorded more than $345M in impairment and restructuring charges for Q4. Adjusted operating income was $129M, with a margin of 22%.

For the three months ended December 31, the company reported a net loss of $284.5M, or $6.38 loss per share, up from a loss of $21.8M, or $0.50 loss per share, for the same period last year.

On an adjusted basis, it earned $64 per share, missing the average Wall Street analyst expectation of $1.05 apiece.

Revenue fell nearly 12% to $599.3M and also missed estimates.

Shares of the company rose nearly 7% in premarket trading and pared most gains before the opening bell.

data.title: U.S. industrial production advances more than expected in January
data.description: U.S. industrial production grew 0.5% M/M in January, outpacing the +0.3% consensus and trailing December's +1.0%, according to data released by the Federal Reserve on Friday.
